title: Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston celebrates its 200-year anniversary as America's oldest continuously operating restaurant.
Established in 1826, the restaurant is a National Historic Landmark known for serving a million oysters annually.
Owner Joseph Milano highlights the historical significance and famous guests, including presidents and celebrities, who frequent the restaurant.

title: Boston’s biggest summer events collide over the next 8 days. Buckle up.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston hosts major events including the Boston Pops Fireworks, a World Cup quarterfinal, and Sail Boston's Parade of Sail.
The Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular on July 4 includes artists like Chance the Rapper and will affect traffic near the Charles River Esplanade.
Gillette Stadium in Foxborough will host its final World Cup match on July 9, with potential traffic disruptions on Route 1.

title: The presidents, celebrities, and famous regulars of Boston’s Union Oyster House
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House, the oldest continuously operating restaurant in the U.S., has hosted notable figures over the decades.
Six U.S. presidents, including Obama and Kennedy, have dined at Boston's historic Union Oyster House.
A 2003 painting by Stan Kotzen features over 50 celebrities who visited Union Oyster House at their peak fame.

title: N.H. State Police announce unexpected death of 19-year veteran
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sgt. Brian J. Ross, a 19-year veteran of the New Hampshire State Police, died unexpectedly at age 41.
Ross served as commander of the Collision Analysis and Reconstruction Unit and worked in various other police roles.
A fund has been created to support Ross's family, and services are scheduled for July 6 and 7.

title: Jaylen Brown says Celtics showed ‘lack of respect’ after trade to Sixers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown expressed dissatisfaction with his trade to the 76ers, citing a lack of respect from the Celtics.
Celtics' Brad Stevens faced criticism from Brown for poor communication during the trade process.
The Celtics traded Brown in a deal involving Paul George, plus two first-round and two second-round picks.

title: Hull man dies after apparent drowning at Nantasket Beach, officials say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Robert Nolan, 76, from Hull was found unresponsive after an apparent drowning at Nantasket Beach on Wednesday evening.
Plymouth County District Attorney's office confirmed Nolan's death at 8:40 p.m.; no foul play is suspected.
The Office of the Chief Medical Examiner is examining Nolan's body for more details on the cause of death.

title: Don Sweeney, Bruins’ retooling plans remain a work in progress
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins traded goalie Joonas Korpisalo to the Rangers for cap relief and acquired Will Borgen for draft picks.
Boston re-signed Connor Clifton and Jordan Harris, focusing on strengthening their defensive lineup during free agency.
GM Don Sweeney aims to manage cap space and improve the roster without overcommitting financially, maintaining flexibility for future moves.

title: Parking will be free at state parks, beaches for Fourth of July weekend
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Free parking at all state parks and beaches announced for July 3-5, marking the 250th American Revolution anniversary.
Governor Maura Healey confirmed the parking waiver applies across all Department of Conservation and Recreation sites.
Some North Shore beaches closed due to sewage spills; successful installation of bypass pipe reported by state officials.

title: What’s up with those online ads for gas-saving gadgets?
author: John Paul
bullets: Gas-saving gadgets advertised online are typically ineffective and unverifiable, according to AAA Northeast's Car Doctor, John Paul.
A 2022 Volkswagen Atlas's sporadic warm air issue may involve vent temperature sensor or system calibration problems.
Run-flat tires provide safety benefits but can lead to a firmer ride and higher replacement costs than conventional tires.

title: Boston civilian board recommends changes to BPD policies on bodycam footage, federal entanglements
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston's Office of Police Accountability and Transparency recommends releasing bodycam footage of critical incidents within 30 days.
OPAT suggests Boston Police Department end collaborations with federal agencies like the FBI and ICE.
Memo advises limiting the sharing of biometric data on non-felony arrestees to federal systems.
